# Source Card: Outcomes of Isolated Neutropenia Referred to Pediatric Hematology-Oncology Clinic

## Summary
According to PubMed, this single-institution retrospective chart review ([DOI](https://doi.org/10.1542/peds.2019-3637), PMID 32883808) of 155 children referred to a pediatric hematology-oncology clinic for isolated neutropenia (ANC <1500/μL) establishes an operational four-band ANC severity scheme (mild/moderate/severe/very severe) and shows that most referred children resolve without subspecialty intervention. For the pediatric CDS module it supplies (a) a source-supported neutropenia definition cutoff and severity banding, and (b) natural-history/outcome context relevant to referral triggers. The severity bands are this study's operational categorization, not a ratified consensus standard.

## Population / assay / lifecycle notes
- Population: children referred to pediatric hematology-oncology for isolated neutropenia; MeSH age coverage spans Infant, Newborn / Infant / Child, Preschool / Child / Adolescent (both sexes). Not gestational-age or postnatal-age stratified; no neonatal-specific bands.
- Assay: ANC from CBC with differential; the source does not specify analyzer or differential method, so bands are stated as not method-dependent by the authors — a CDS implementation should not assume cross-analyzer equivalence.
- Lifecycle: published 2020-09 (Pediatrics 146(4)). This is a primary study's operational scheme, not a versioned guideline; no supersession relationship asserted.

## Scope-exit / referral relevance (if any)
- The source is framed around the referral boundary itself (children referred to peds heme-onc for isolated neutropenia). It supports a natural-history finding that most referred children do not progress in severity and do not require subspecialty intervention/hospitalization, and that Black children have higher odds of persistent mild neutropenia (benign ethnic neutropenia consideration). These inform referral-trigger context but are NOT executable referral rules; they are proposals for the CDS converter to weigh, not directives.

## Limitations & conflicts
- Single-center retrospective cohort (N=155) with referral-population selection bias; findings may not generalize to primary-care screening populations.
- The four ANC severity bands are this study's operational categorization, not a consensus/guideline standard. Severity-band breakpoints are not universally standardized across pediatric sources; do NOT collapse or average this scheme with differently-defined banding from other sources — preserve as a named conflict pending a designated authoritative standard.
- Full text was not retrievable from PubMed Central; all numeric thresholds here are quoted verbatim from the published abstract (Methods/Results). Any figure/table-level threshold detail beyond the abstract is NOT locatable from the retrieved source and is therefore not asserted.
- No neonatal- or gestational-age-specific thresholds are provided in this source; missingness for neonatal populations is explicit, not inferred.
- No diagnosis/treatment/dosing/transfusion directive is asserted; G-CSF utilization is reported only as a descriptive outcome statistic.
